avalancheActivity.highlights: Further increase in danger of dry avalanches as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall above approximately 1000 m and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size in the course of the day. Towards the southeast the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
Especially on very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes numerous medium-sized and, in many cases, large natural avalanches are to be expected. Here the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach very large size.
In the typical avalanche paths in particular the avalanches can reach as far as the valley bottom and endanger transportation routes that are exposed.
The conditions are very precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities outside marked and open pistes.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches, including large ones. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and up to 80 cm in some localities, will fall until late in the night above approximately 1500 m. Over a wide area 10 to 25 cm of snow will fall until Tuesday above approximately 1000 m. On Monday the wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. Until Tuesday the wind will be strong to storm force over a wide area.
The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall will become increasingly prone to triggering.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Several medium-sized to large natural dry avalanches have been released as the day progresses. Several slab avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a gradual strengthening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and stormy weather a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Some snow will fall until midday in particular along the border with France, along the border between Valais and Italy.

The strong wind will transport the new snow. As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. This applies in particular in gullies and bowls and in areas close to the tree line, and in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

On wind-loaded slopes and on very steep slopes medium-sized to large slab avalanches are possible, in isolated cases also in case of releases originating from steep and high-altitude southeast facing starting zones that have retained the snow thus far.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions over a wide area 25 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday. Some snow will fall until midday.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in danger of dry avalanches as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
High-altitude starting zones will be replenished further as a consequence of snowfall and wind.
Especially on very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.
In some places the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 40 cm of snow will fall until late in the night above approximately 1600 m. Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall until Tuesday above approximately 1200 m. The wind will be strong to storm force.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a slow strengthening of the snowpack.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

bulletins.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a very dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are being formed by the strong westerly wind can be released easily or naturally in all aspects above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are to be found also in areas close to the tree line. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

Avalanches can in some cases penetrate deep layers and reach large size. This applies on west, north and east facing slopes. Caution is to be exercised in particular in leeward starting zones that have retained the snow thus far.

On very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
15 to 30 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday. 15 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Wednesday. The wind will be strong to storm force.

The fresh snow and in particular the extensive wind slabs will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.
Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The conditions are very dangerous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities. As a consequence of the strong westerly wind more natural avalanches are possible.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and wind, wind slabs formed. They must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. They can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs remain prone to triggering in all aspects. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions gave rise to gradual consolidation of the snowpack in some places in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The strong wind will transport the new snow. Fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. They are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.

In very isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 10 to 20 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1000 m. Little snow will fall in the late morning in some localities.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs in the high Alpine regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a gathering strong wind from westerly directions, clearly visible wind slabs will form at elevated altitudes.
Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant, especially on steep shady slopes in high Alpine regions, as well as on wind-loaded slopes. Caution is to be exercised at transitions into gullies and bowls.

In isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

As a consequence of falling temperatures, the activity of moist and wet avalanches will decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the last few days hard wind slabs formed over a wide area. The sometimes storm force wind will transport the loosely bonded old snow. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
As a consequence of a moderate wind from northwesterly directions, mostly small wind slabs will form.

Low and intermediate altitudes: As a consequence of falling temperatures and the strong westerly wind, a crust will form on the surface during the night.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind will be strong to storm force at times. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind will transport the fresh and old snow. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form in the course of the day. These will form in particular between approximately 2100 and 2500 m.
The wind slabs are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. They can be released even by a single winter sport participant.
Caution is to be exercised in particular on slopes, and on wind-loaded slopes in particular above the tree line.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 25 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1400 m. The strong wind will transport the new snow significantly.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent. The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in some places be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 1800 m. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Isolated whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger.

In addition the mostly small wind slabs in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are prone to triggering. This applies in particular above approximately 2000 m. They are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall. As a consequence of a strong wind from northwesterly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs will form especially in gullies and bowls. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places.

Above approximately 1800 m: The snowpack will be prone to triggering. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack represent the main danger. In addition the fresh wind slabs should be taken into account.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released by people. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but easily released. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line, and on little used shady slopes. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.
The wind was strong at times in some regions, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant. Caution is to be exercised at their margins in particular.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack remains generally prone to triggering. Distinct weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
At high altitude the wind slabs have increased in size additionally in the last two days.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will persist. Above the tree line a treacherous avalanche situation will prevail.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence. Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind will be strong at times. Over a wide area large surface-area wind slabs will form. These will form in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. In particular on very steep shady slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended. Experience and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment: Above approximately 1900 m sometimes easily released wind slabs formed. Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible. In particular transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are treacherous. Mostly small natural avalanches are possible below approximately 1800 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In some places wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

bulletins.highlights: Individual natural avalanches are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The off-piste conditions are dangerous. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found also in areas close to the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

As a consequence of new snow and a strong northwesterly wind, easily released wind slabs will form in particular above the tree line, in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Individual natural avalanches are to be expected.

In the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 4 (high) the avalanche situation is more dangerous. Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday. 5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Wednesday.
As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places.
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

tendency.highlights: The conditions remain favourable. Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Caution and restraint are recommended.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies especially on steep shady slopes adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line.
As a consequence of the strong northerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. These are in some cases prone to triggering. In some cases avalanches are medium-sized.

Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint. Only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack. The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

Low and intermediate altitudes: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche-prone wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Avalanches can be released, mostly by large additional loads.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs remain prone to triggering. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions gave rise to gradual consolidation of the snowpack in some cases.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released in isolated cases in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the mostly small wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.
